Key Findings

  • EfficientNet-B0 (Pretrained) wins at 97.7% accuracy with only 5.3M parameters — best accuracy-to-size ratio.
  • Transfer learning adds +1.3% over the best scratch model (ResNet/MobileNet at 96.4%), confirming ImageNet features transfer to medical imaging.
  • Custom CNN baseline (87.5%) has clinically dangerous recall gaps: glioma 84.4% and meningioma 80.7% — below the 90% screening threshold.
  • MobileNet matches ResNet scratch (96.4% each) with ~3.5x fewer parameters (3.2M vs 11.2M) — strong candidate for edge deployment.
  • All models except Custom CNN pass the 90% per-class recall threshold for clinical screening acceptability.

Head-to-Head Comparison

ModelTypeParams AccuracyF1 MacroROC-AUC Min RecallClinical?
Custom CNN Scratch ~1.2M 87.5% 0.8749 0.9718 0.807 (meningioma) FAIL
ResNet (Scratch) Scratch ~11.2M 96.4% 0.9639 0.9977 0.941 (meningioma) Pass
MobileNet (Scratch) Scratch ~3.2M 96.4% 0.9639 0.9973 0.944 (notumor) Pass
ResNet (Pretrained) Transfer ~11.7M 96.8% 0.9676 0.9974 0.952 (meningioma) Pass
EfficientNet-B0 (Pretrained) Transfer ~5.3M 97.7% 0.9769 0.9990 0.959 (notumor) Pass

Dataset: Kaggle Brain Tumor MRI (7,023 images, 4 classes) · Test set: 1,080 images · Augmentation: Conservative
